INC., TY FIVE STAR CORPORATION AND VALLEY POINT NURSING REHAB, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. " '[A]LLEGATIONS OF FRAUD INVOLVE A SERIOUS ATTACK ON CHARACTER, AND FAIRNESS TO THE DEFENDANT DEMANDS THAT HE SHOULD RECEIVE THE FULLEST POSSIBLE DETAILS OF THE CHARGE IN ORDER TO PREPARE HIS DEFENSE.'" (COMMITTEE ON CHILDREN'S TELEVISION, INC. V. GENERAL FOODS CORP. (1983) 35 CAL.3D 197, 216.) PLAINTIFF IS CORRECT THAT FRAUDULENT INTENT "HAS BEEN INFERRED FROM SUCH CIRCUMSTANCES AS DEFENDANT'S INSOLVENCY, HIS HASTY REPUDIATION OF THE PROMISE, HIS FAILURE EVEN TO ATTEMPT PERFORMANCE, OR HIS CONTINUED ASSURANCES AFTER IT WAS CLEAR HE WOULD NOT PERFORM." (TENZER V. SUPERSCOPE, INC. (1985) 39 CAL.3D 18, 30.) NEVERTHELESS, PLAINTIFF DOES NOT PLEAD WITH SPECIFICITY WHEN SUCH ASSURANCES OCCURRED OR WHEN IT BECAME CLEAR THAT FULL PERFORMANCE WOULD NOT OCCUR. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO "ALLEGE THE NAMES OF THE PERSONS WHO MADE THE ALLEGEDLY FRAUDULENT REPRESENTATIONS, THEIR AUTHORITY TO SPEAK, TO WHOM THEY SPOKE, WHAT THEY SAID OR WROTE, AND WHEN IT WAS SAID OR WRITTEN." (TARMANN V. STATE FARM MUT. AUTO. INS. CO. (1991) 2 CAL.APP.4TH 153, 157.). ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO NURSING CENTER INC., MCCLURE REHABILITATION CENTER INC., SAN RAFAEL CARE CENTER INC., JAMES PREIMESBERGER, VALLEY POINT NIRSING REHAB, INC., SAN BRUNO SKILLED NURSING HOSPITAL, INC. AND CONVALESCENT CENTER, MISSION ST. INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. IN RULING ON A MOTION TO STRIKE, THE ALLEGATIONS OF A COMPLAINT ARE CONSIDERED AS A WHOLE AND ARE PRESUMED TO BE TRUE. (CLAUSON V. SUPERIOR COURT (1998) 67 CAL.APP.4TH 1253, 1255.) PLAINTIFF, NEVERTHELESS, DOES NOT SUFFICIENTLY PLEAD FACTS AMOUNTING TO OPPRESSION, FRAUD, OR MALICE. "THE MERE ALLEGATION AN INTENTIONAL TORT WAS COMMITTED IS NOT SUFFICIENT TO WARRANT AN AWARD OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES. [CITATIONS.] NOT ONLY MUST THERE BE CIRCUMSTANCES OF OPPRESSION, FRAUD OR MALICE, BUT FACTS MUST BE ALLEGED IN THE PLEADING TO SUPPORT SUCH A CLAIM. (GRIEVES V. SUPERIOR COURT (1984) 157 CAL.APP.3D 159, 166.) PLAINTIFF FAILS TO MAKE THIS THRESHOLD SHOWING IN ITS FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. A "CONCLUSORY CHARACTERIZATION OF [A] DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT AS INTENTIONAL, WILLFUL AND FRAUDULENT IS A PATENTLY INSUFFICIENT STATEMENT OF 'OPPRESSION, FRAUD, OR MALICE, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED,' WITHIN THE MEANING OF SECTION 3294" TO SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. (BROUSSEAU V. JARRETT (1977) 73 CAL.APP.3D 864, 872.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489; DEPT. 302. =302/EHG

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-12-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS MCCLURE REHABILITATION CENTER INC., SAN RAFAEL CARE CENTER INC., JAMES PREIMESBERGER, SAN BRUNO SKILLED NURSING HOSPITAL, INC., CONVALESCENT CENTER, MISSION ST. INC., TY FIVE STAR CORPORATION and VALLEY POINT NURSING REHAB, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendant's demurrer to Plaintiff's cause of action for fraud is sustained with leave to amend. " '[A]llegations of fraud involve a serious attack on character, and fairness to the defendant demands that he should receive the fullest possible details of the charge in order to prepare his defense.'" (Committee on Children's Television, Inc. v. General Foods Corp. (1983) 35 Cal.3d 197, 216.) Plaintiff is correct that fraudulent intent "has been inferred from such circumstances as defendant's insolvency, his hasty repudiation of the promise, his failure even to attempt performance, or his continued assurances after it was clear he would not perform." (Tenzer v. Superscope, Inc. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 18, 30.) Nevertheless, plaintiff does not plead with specificity when such assurances occurred or when it became clear that full performance would not occur. Plaintiff fails to "allege the names of the persons who made the allegedly fraudulent representations, their authority to speak, to whom they spoke, what they said or wrote, and when it was said or written." (Tarmann v. State Farm Mut. Auto. Ins. Co. (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 153, 157.). Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

Off Calendar Nov-12-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO NURSING CENTER INC., MCCLURE REHABILITATION CENTER INC., SAN RAFAEL CARE CENTER INC., JAMES PREIMESBERGER, VALLEY POINT NIRSING REHAB, INC., SAN BRUNO SKILLED NURSING HOSPITAL, INC. and CONVALESCENT CENTER, MISSION ST. INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted. In ruling on a motion to strike, the allegations of a complaint are considered as a whole and are presumed to be true. (Clauson v. Superior Court (1998) 67 Cal.App.4th 1253, 1255.) Plaintiff, nevertheless, does not sufficiently plead facts amounting to oppression, fraud, or malice. "The mere allegation an intentional tort was committed is not sufficient to warrant an award of punitive damages. [Citations.] Not only must there be circumstances of oppression, fraud or malice, but facts must be alleged in the pleading to support such a claim. (Grieves v. Superior Court (1984) 157 Cal.App.3d 159, 166.) Plaintiff fails to make this threshold showing in its First Amended Complaint. A "conclusory characterization of [a] defendant's conduct as intentional, willful and fraudulent is a patently insufficient statement of 'oppression, fraud, or malice, express or implied,' within the meaning of section 3294" to support a claim for punitive damages. (Brousseau v. Jarrett (1977) 73 Cal.App.3d 864, 872.) Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
